remote_base.role: the one remote source site that seeds the persisted pull base

local_edited.role: the one local edited site that produces the candidate plan

remote_changed.role: the same remote site after independent drift between dry-run and apply

runner.role: the only process allowed to compare, upload, inspect, and recover

test_topology.remote_base: one source site that seeds the persisted pull base
test_topology.local_edited: one imported local site with user edits
test_topology.remote_changed: the same remote site after independent drift
test_topology.runner: the only actor allowed to run the push protocol

topology: remote-base is one WordPress source site on the private network
local-edited is one imported WordPress clone with user edits
remote-changed is the same source site after live drift
runner is the only caller that can run preflight, snapshot listing, dry-run, apply, journal inspect, and recovery
evidence: push_preflight mints the push session and auth scope before any planning call
push_snapshot_hashes captures the live remote comparison set used for dry-run planning
push_plan_dry_run is eligibility only and never a write lock
push_batch_apply revalidates the live remote before every batch and again at the storage boundary
push_journal and push_recover inspect read durable evidence before any mutating recovery mode
shape: remote-base pulls first and seeds the merge base
local-edited is restored from that base and edited independently
runner calls push_preflight, push_snapshot_hashes, push_plan_dry_run, push_batch_apply, push_journal, and push_recover against the same remote site as it drifts
push_plan_dry_run is only an eligibility receipt; push_batch_apply must revalidate live remote state before every batch and again at the storage boundary
push_journal and push_recover inspect are read-only evidence readers, while mutating recovery modes require fresh live proof
remote-db and local-db remain isolated so live drift does not contaminate the local edit history
use only the sandbox-provided 8080 ingress through a local-only proxy when browser-visible inspection is needed
never use remote tunnels such as ngrok, cloudflared, localtunnel, serveo, localhost.run, or Tailscale Funnel

topology: remote-base is the source blueprint that becomes the persisted pull base
local-edited is the imported clone with local edits
remote-changed is the same source blueprint after independent drift
runner is the local test process that drives the push protocol against the disposable blueprints
evidence: remote-base seeds the persisted pull base package and push identity evidence
local-edited supplies the edited local clone used to build the canonical plan
remote-changed is the same remote site observed later so stale apply can be rejected
the runner stays the only actor that may compare, upload, inspect, or recover
shape: remote-base seeds the pull base and push identity evidence
local-edited is the imported site after local changes
remote-changed is the same remote site after it drifts independently and forces stale-apply rejection and recovery
the runner proves dry-run and apply are separate by taking a fresh snapshot listing, uploading a dry-run plan, then applying only after live revalidation succeeds
the runner keeps the three blueprints isolated and does not open a public tunnel
use only the sandbox-provided 8080 ingress through a local-only proxy when browser-visible inspection is needed
